---

## What you need to do to deploy:

You need to fix Git authentication first. The quickest option:

### Option A — GitHub CLI (easiest)
```bash
brew install gh
gh auth login
git push origin main
```

### Option B — Switch to SSH
```bash
# Check for existing SSH key
ls ~/.ssh/id_ed25519.pub

# If exists, switch remote to SSH:
git remote set-url origin git@github.com:sg-satoshi/saabai-site.git
git push origin main
```

### Option C — Personal Access Token
1. Go to https://github.com/settings/tokens → Generate new token (check `repo` scope)
2. Run:
```bash
git remote set-url origin https://YOUR_TOKEN@github.com/sg-satoshi/saabai-site.git
git push origin main
```

Once `git push` succeeds, Vercel will auto-deploy within ~2 minutes and the dark theme changes will go live on saabai.ai.

## 6. `app/globals.css` — Add the `admin-theme` dark scope (add at bottom of file)

```css
/* ─── Admin / Dashboard dark theme scope ───────────────────────────────── */

.admin-theme {
  --saabai-bg:              #0b0f1a;
  --saabai-surface:         #111827;
  --saabai-surface-raised:  #1f2937;
  --saabai-nav:             #0b0f1a;

  --saabai-border:          #374151;
  --saabai-border-accent:   #06b6d440;

  --saabai-text:            #f9fafb;
  --saabai-text-muted:      #9ca3af;
  --saabai-text-dim:        #6b7280;

  background-color: #0b0f1a;
  color: #f9fafb;
}
```

---

## After applying all patches

```bash
npm run build
```

The `.admin-theme` scope overrides the CSS variables only within those page wrappers, so the public marketing site stays white while admin/dashboard/login pages get the dark theme. The `.min-h-screen` global override is removed so it no longer forces white on every page indiscriminately.

Based on the context, here's the theming strategy for saabai-site: ## Dark Theme (Public-facing pages) / Light Theme (Admin pages) ### Public pages that should be **dark** (dark blue background): - `/` (home) - `/about`, `/services`, `/advisory`, `/process` - `/for-accounting-firms`, `/for-law-firms`, `/for-real-estate` - `/lex`, `/plon`, `/tributum` - `/case-studies`, `/use-cases`, `/insights` - `/faq`, `/calculator`, `/pulse` - `/rex-widget`, `/lex-widget` - `/rex-changelog` ### Admin/portal pages that should be **light**: - `/saabai-admin` and sub-pages (`/social/instagram`, `/social/linkedin`, `/subscribers`) - `/client-portal` - `/login` - `/mission-control` - `/rex-dashboard` - `/rex-analytics` - `/atlas-memory-control` --- ## How to Implement The cleanest approach is to use CSS classes on the `` or layout wrappers: ### 1. **Root layout** (`app/layout.tsx`) — set dark as default ```tsx {children} ``` ### 2. **Admin layouts** — override to light Create or update a shared admin layout that overrides: ```tsx // app/saabai-admin/layout.tsx
{children}
``` Same for `/rex-dashboard`, `/mission-control`, `/client-portal`, `/rex-analytics`, `/atlas-memory-control`, `/login`. ### 3. **Using `data-theme` attribute** (alternative) In `globals.css`: ```css :root { --bg: #0a0f2e; --fg: #ffffff; } [data-theme="light"] { --bg: #ffffff; --fg: #111111; } ``` Then wrap admin pages: `
`. --- ## Quickest Path Since `app/layout.tsx` is the root layout, set it dark by default. Then for each admin page/group, wrap the content in a `div` with a light background class.
